title: mentha arvensis var. legitima
intro: Mentha arvensis var. legitima, also known as wild mint or corn mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis var. legitima include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, relieve stress and anxiety, and help with respiratory problems such as colds and coughs. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
